steps.title: Identify what the blank must express
steps.content: The passage contrasts a misleading situation ("only a few users") with the actual situation ("over 8,000 users"). The blank describes 8,000 ratings as a quantity that justifies trusting the average. The word needed means "enough" or "sufficient."

steps.title: Substitute each choice and eliminate
steps.content:
• "excessive": "Excessive" means too much or more than needed. Calling 8,000 ratings excessive contradicts the passage's point that more ratings increase reliability. ✗
  • "adequate": "Adequate" means sufficient or enough — exactly the contrast the passage draws between a mere handful of ratings (unreliable) and 8,000 ratings (enough to trust the average). ✓
  • "accurate": "Accurate" describes correctness, not quantity. The passage compares few users to many users, not incorrect numbers to correct ones. ✗
  • "insufficient": "Insufficient" means not enough, which directly contradicts the passage's claim that 8,000 ratings support confidence in the average. ✗
That matches the correct answer.
